and were most commonly found in churches . the earliest examples come from the thirteenth century but they were popular up until the seventeenth century and then again in the victorian gothic revival . surviving brasses from the medieval period are limited due to the turbulent history of the church but they do survive in considerable numbers in the east of england , germany and flanders . made from an alloy of copper and zinc , a material known as latten , they were laid into church floors and walls . monumental brasses are historically and stylistically significant because they record dress , architecture , armoury , heraldry ( coats of arms and insignia ) and palaeography ( handwriting )
